pdf_text: The Wellcome Trust Seed Grant provides funding for early career researchers. Grants of up to fifty thousand pounds sterling are available to researchers based in low and middle income countries. Applicants must hold a doctoral degree and be employed by an eligible institution. The grant supports research in global health, infectious disease, and mental health. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis throughout the year. The programme particularly encourages proposals from researchers in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. Projects must be completed within twenty four months of the start date. Indirect costs are capped at fifteen percent of direct project costs. All publications arising from the grant must acknowledge Wellcome Trust funding. Financial reports are required every six months during the project period. Applications must be submitted through the Wellcome Trust grants portal. Queries should be directed to the grants management team by email. The foundation does not fund infrastructure or building projects. Collaborative projects involving two or more institutions are strongly encouraged. Priority is given to projects addressing neglected tropical diseases. Researchers from eligible countries may apply as principal investigator. Co-investigators from high income countries may participate but may not lead. A full budget breakdown is required with all applications for funding. Successful applicants will be notified within three months of submission.
